content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Added reconciliation-retry bypass that skips rebase_task_worktree and ensure_project when the run snapshot is already Completed. Both task processors (process_task_multi_repo and process_task) now read the run snapshot before rebase; if RunStatus::Completed, they skip rebase and ensure_project entirely, falling through directly to dispatch (which returns Ok(()) for Completed) and then to try_reconcile_success. This prevents rebase conflicts from blocking reconciliation-only retries after transient br/milestone failures. Added a regression test (dispatch_completed_run_returns_ok_for_reconciliation_retry) verifying that dispatch_in_worktree_with_service returns Ok(()) for RunStatus::Completed.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added is_reconciliation_retry check to process_task_multi_repo (daemon_loop.rs:681-695): reads run snapshot via ProjectId + read_run_snapshot; if RunStatus::Completed, wraps rebase_task_worktree and ensure_project in if !is_reconciliation_retry block. Uses repo_root for the snapshot read.
2. [Completed] Added identical is_reconciliation_retry check to process_task (daemon_loop.rs:1431-1463): same pattern using base_dir (single-repo path) for the snapshot read. Wraps rebase_task_worktree and ensure_project in if !is_reconciliation_retry block.
3. [Completed] Added regression test dispatch_completed_run_returns_ok_for_reconciliation_retry in daemon_loop.rs test module (behind #[cfg(all(test, feature="test-stub"))]): constructs minimal AgentExecutionService with StubBackendAdapter, calls dispatch_in_worktree_with_service with RunStatus::Completed, asserts Ok(()). Validates the round 13 fix that prevents the permanent fail/retry/fail loop.
4. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt, cargo test (757 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (0 new warnings), cargo fmt --check (clean). Also ran the regression test specifically via cargo test --features test-stub --lib (1 passed).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 757 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored (full suite)
- cargo test --features test-stub --lib -- dispatch_completed_run: 1 passed (regression test)
- cargo fmt --check: no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: 0 new warnings in modified files (16 pre-existing warnings unchanged)
- Multi-repo path verified: is_reconciliation_retry at daemon_loop.rs:686-695 reads run snapshot via repo_root; if Completed, skips rebase (lines 697-724) and ensure_project (lines 726-754)
- Single-repo path verified: is_reconciliation_retry at daemon_loop.rs:1436-1444 reads run snapshot via base_dir; if Completed, skips rebase (lines 1447-1456) and ensure_project (lines 1458-1462)
- Safety: read_run_snapshot returns Err on first-run (no snapshot file), so is_reconciliation_retry correctly defaults to false for normal task dispatches, preserving the existing rebase+ensure_project flow
- Idempotency chain confirmed: reconciliation-retry skips rebase -> dispatch returns Ok(()) for Completed -> try_reconcile_success runs all idempotent steps (br close checks status, sync unconditional, record_bead_completion handles replays)

## Outstanding Risks

- The is_reconciliation_retry check reads the run snapshot from disk on every task dispatch. This is a cheap FS read and the snapshot is small, but it adds one extra I/O per task cycle. Acceptable since the alternative (rebase failure blocking reconciliation) is far worse.
- If the run snapshot file exists but is corrupted (partial write), read_run_snapshot returns Err, which means is_reconciliation_retry defaults to false and the normal rebase path runs. This is safe but means a corrupted snapshot after a Completed run would still attempt rebase on retry.
